CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE remote_table (id INT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Name 1');
INSERT INTO remote_table (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Remote Description 1');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM remote_table WHERE description LIKE '%Remote%');
SELECT a.id, a.name, b.description FROM test_table a JOIN remote_table b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table) AS total_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM remote_table) AS remote_count;
SELECT a.id, a.name FROM test_table a WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM remote_table b WHERE a.id = b.id) AND a.name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COALESCE((SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = 1), 'No Name Found') AS result_name;
WITH remote_data AS (SELECT id, description FROM remote_table WHERE description IS NOT NULL) SELECT a.id, a.name, r.description FROM test_table a LEFT JOIN remote_data r ON a.id = r.id;
SELECT a.id, a.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM remote_table b WHERE a.id = b.id) AS related_count FROM test_table a;
INSERT INTO remote_table (id, description) SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM remote_table);
DELETE FROM remote_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name IS NULL);
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id > 0;
SELECT * FROM temp_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM remote_table WHERE temp_table.id = remote_table.id);
UPDATE test_table SET name = (SELECT description FROM remote_table WHERE remote_table.id = test_table.id) WHERE test_table.id IN (SELECT id FROM remote_table);
